inputPrompt:
# Relay stage 3: Diagnose
Task: 00c-fix-task-author-read-only
Working directory: /Users/admin/Dev/temp-visual-relay/

## Task input
# Make Fix-Task Authoring Read-Only

## Problem

`FixTaskAuthorRunner` is described as a read-only prompt-and-parse step:

> No worktree - this is a read-only prompt-and-parse step.

But its synthetic stage definition currently grants broad capability:

- `Files: "all"`
- `Commands: "all"`

That gives the task-authoring model more power than the feature needs. It only needs the failure
context already assembled into the prompt and should return JSON markdown for a new task. It should
not be able to edit the repository or execute arbitrary commands.

## Goal

Make "Create task to fix" authoring genuinely read-only and low-capability while preserving the button
behavior and task-writing flow owned by the app.

## What to build

1. Reduce `FixTaskAuthorRunner` stage capabilities.
   - Use no write access.
   - Use no command execution unless a narrowly justified read-only command set is truly necessary.
   - Prefer passing all needed failure context in the prompt, as it does today.
2. Keep the app-owned write step unchanged.
   - The app may still write the returned markdown through `RelayTaskWriter.CreateAsync` after it
     validates and disambiguates the slug.
   - The subagent itself must not write files.
3. Add tests that inspect the captured `StageInvocation`.
   - The fix-task author invocation must not use `Files: "all"` or `Commands: "all"`.
   - The happy path still creates a new task from returned JSON.
   - Invalid JSON/error paths still surface a clean status message and write nothing.
4. Keep the behavior general. Do not add language- or toolchain-specific logic to the authoring path.

## Done criteria

- The fix-task authoring subagent cannot edit files or run arbitrary commands.
- Existing create-fix-task tests still pass.
- New capability-scope tests fail before the fix and pass after it.
- The full `./visual-relay check` gate passes.


## Manifest
(not set yet)

All needed failure context is already in the prompt. Tests must inspect the captured StageInvocation to confirm Files and Commands are not \"all\", and existing create-fix-task tests must still pass.",

## Stage 2 - Research

{
  "findings": "FixTaskAuthorRunner.cs (lines 64-72) creates a synthetic RelayStageDefinition with Files=\"all\" and Commands=\"all\", contradicting its own doc comment ('read-only prompt-and-parse step'). All failure context is already assembled into the prompt via FailedRunContextReader.Read() + BuildPrompt(), so the subagent needs zero file/command access. FixTaskFakeRunner and GatedFixTaskRunner (tests/VisualRelay.Tests/FixTaskFakeRunners.cs) don't capture the StageInvocation — they need an Invocation property for capability assertions. The app-owned write step (RelayTaskWriter.CreateAsync in MainWindowViewModel.FixTask.cs:117) stays untouched. Existing tests use a partial-class pattern (MainWindowViewModelFixTaskTests.cs + .Execution.cs). The RelayStageDefinition record (Domain) uses string fields Files and Commands with conventions 'none'/'all'/'some'. Stage 1 (Ideate) uses 'none' for both — the same values should apply here.",
  "constraints": [
    "Do NOT modify the app-owned write step (RelayTaskWriter.CreateAsync in MainWindowViewModel.FixTask.cs line 117).",
    "Do NOT add language- or toolchain-specific logic to the authoring path.",
    "Existing create-fix-task tests must continue to pass without changes.",
    "The fix-task author subagent must have no file write access and no command execution capability.",
    "New tests must inspect the captured StageInvocation to assert Files != \"all\" and Commands != \"all\".",
    "Invalid/error paths must surface clean status messages and write no files.",
    "The full `./visual-relay check` gate must pass as the final done-criteria.",
    "All tests run under [Collection(\"Headless\")] Avalonia test collection.",
    "FixTaskAuthorRunner.RunAsync already passes all needed context through the prompt parameter — no additional data plumbing is required."
  ]
}
